# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

TMP_DIR="/tmp"
TMP_BASE="myapp-$$"
TMP_FILE="${TMP_DIR}/${TMP_BASE}.tmp"
TMP_OUT="${TMP_DIR}/${TMP_BASE}.out"

cleanup() {
  rm -f -- "$TMP_FILE" "$TMP_OUT"
}
trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM HUP

umask 077

if [[ -e "$TMP_FILE" || -e "$TMP_OUT" ]]; then
  echo "Temp file already exists: $TMP_FILE or $TMP_OUT" >&2
  exit 1
fi

: > "$TMP_FILE"
: > "$TMP_OUT"

if [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; then
  cat -- "$@" > "$TMP_FILE"
else
  cat > "$TMP_FILE"
fi

tr -d '\r' < "$TMP_FILE" > "$TMP_OUT"
mv -f -- "$TMP_OUT" "$TMP_FILE"
: > "$TMP_OUT"

awk '
  { gsub(/[[:space:]]+/, " "); sub(/^ /, ""); sub(/ $/, ""); }
  NF { print }
' < "$TMP_FILE" > "$TMP_OUT"
mv -f -- "$TMP_OUT" "$TMP_FILE"
: > "$TMP_OUT"

sort < "$TMP_FILE" > "$TMP_OUT"
mv -f -- "$TMP_OUT" "$TMP_FILE"

cat -- "$TMP_FILE"
